title: Sour Clam Brunch Stew
description: This Portuguese-inspired brunch stew is tender and sour, perfect for a light lunch or as part of a brunch spread. It's pescatarian-friendly, featuring clams in a flavorful sauce.
instructions: Blanch the clams in salted water until open, discard any that do not open.
Dice the onions and garlic into small pieces. In a large pot, sauté them in olive oil until translucent.
Add chopped tomatoes to the pot with the onions and garlic, let simmer for about 5 minutes to release flavors.
Stir in vegetable broth, white wine vinegar, and sour cream, bring to a boil then reduce heat to low. Simmer gently for about 10-12 minutes or until clams are tender.
Add the opened clams directly into the pot, stir well to coat them with the sauce. Let simmer another 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot over crusty bread and garnish with fresh herbs if desired.
